peelOverview: PSLE model answers use the PEEL structure to deliver clear, well-developed responses with a natural conversational rhythm. A typical distinction-level answer starts with a 1-sentence Point directly addressing the prompt, followed by 2-3 sentences of Explanation to provide logical reasons. This is supported by a 3-4 sentence Example, usually a vivid personal anecdote, and wraps up with a 1-sentence Link that confidently connects back to the main question.

peelByQuestionType.questionType: Would you be interested in / would you participate in <activity>?
pointPhrases: Yes, I would definitely be interested in ...
Personally, I would jump at the chance to ...
I think I would be keen on signing up for ...
No, I would politely decline, mainly because ...
explainPhrases: What appeals to me most is ...
This activity will provide me with a chance to ...
examplePhrases: A perfect example of this is ...
If I were to join, I could ...
linkPhrases: All in all, this is an opportunity I would not miss.
So yes, this is something I would love to take part in.
Thus, this is definitely appealing to me.

peelByQuestionType.questionType: Have you ever experienced / done something similar?
pointPhrases: Yes, I have had a similar experience ...
Indeed, I remember a time when ...
There was a time when I ...
One experience that stood out to me was ...
explainPhrases: It was a memorable experience because ...
I learned a lot from this incident as ...
examplePhrases: To illustrate, my friends and I ...
Once, I wanted to try out for ...
linkPhrases: Overall, it was an enriching experience ...
Even now, I can still remember ...
It truly was a memorable event which I will remember for a long time to come.

pointPhrases: I think taking care of this is very important.
Yes, I believe that this plays a crucial role.
In my opinion, this is essential because ...
I definitely think it is vital to ...
explainPhrases: If we do not ..., we will ...
This is because it helps us to ...
Otherwise, there will not be enough resources for us in the future.
examplePhrases: For example, currently a major problem is ...
In my own life, I have noticed that ...
A good illustration of this would be ...
linkPhrases: Therefore, it is vital that we ...
For these reasons, I strongly believe in the importance of ...
Consequently, we must always take care to ...

peelByQuestionType.questionType: Do you agree that / what do you think about <claim>?
pointPhrases: I think that this is generally true.
I do not entirely agree with ...
While some might think so, I personally feel ...
explainPhrases: This is largely due to the fact that ...
On one hand ..., but on the other hand ...
It is important to consider that ...
Some people may be shy, however, I do not share their opinion.
examplePhrases: A good example of this is ...
I have seen this firsthand when ...
linkPhrases: Thus, I stand by the belief that ...
In conclusion, while there are exceptions, I think ...
Therefore, this reflects how important it is to ...

generalOpeners.example: Well, this is certainly a tricky question to answer.
generalOpeners.whenToUse: Buys a beat to think; signals you're considering the question.

generalOpeners.example: In my opinion, recycling should start at home.
generalOpeners.whenToUse: Signals a personal stance without being aggressive.

generalOpeners.example: Personally, I find this activity very meaningful.
generalOpeners.whenToUse: Softens an opinion as your own view.

generalOpeners.example: From my experience, group projects always teach me more.
Honestly speaking, I used to avoid such events.
generalOpeners.whenToUse: Adds candour; good for showing growth or change of mind.

generalOpeners.example: To be frank, I am quite a heavy sleeper.
generalOpeners.whenToUse: Introduces a mild confession or honest personal trait.

generalOpeners.example: What's more, my poem may even be selected to be published.
generalOpeners.whenToUse: Adds a compelling secondary point to your explanation.

generalOpeners.example: Apart from that, I also love taking pictures with my friends.
generalOpeners.whenToUse: Transitions smoothly to another example or reason.

agreeStarters.example: Yes, I would definitely take part in this.
Without a doubt, this is a worthy cause.

agreeStarters.whenToUse: Formal agreement; good after the examiner makes a statement.

agreeStarters.example: I completely agree that children should learn how to cook.
agreeStarters.whenToUse: Directly aligning with the premise of the prompt.

disagreeStarters.example: I would respectfully disagree because the benefits are short-lived.
While I understand the appeal, I think there are downsides too.
disagreeStarters.whenToUse: Acknowledges the other side before objecting.

disagreeStarters.starter: I do not really think that ...
disagreeStarters.example: I do not really think that giving a fruit basket will attract children.
disagreeStarters.whenToUse: Softens a rejection of an idea presented in the stimulus.

disagreeStarters.starter: However, I do not share their opinion.
disagreeStarters.example: Some people may be reluctant to show their work. However, I do not share their opinion.
disagreeStarters.whenToUse: Contrasting your view with a common misconception or opposing view.

personalAnecdote: A few months ago, my family and I ...
In fact, my own grandmother used to ...
This reminds me of an incident at my school ...
Once, I wanted to try out for ...
Before our lives were affected by Covid-19, I watched ...
When I was on the way to school last week, I happened to see ...

hedgesAndQualifiers.meaning: Softens a strong claim — 'perhaps the most important factor is ...'
Partial agreement / nuance — 'I agree, to some extent.'
Avoids absolute claims — 'in most cases, this works well.'
Frames a claim as defensible — 'arguably the best way to ...'
Indicates a broad truth rather than an absolute rule — 'people in Singapore are generally orderly.'

closingMoves.example: All in all, I would jump at the chance.
closingMoves.whenToUse: Standard PEEL Link — recaps and lands the stance.

closingMoves.example: For these reasons, I think this is a valuable initiative.
closingMoves.whenToUse: Loops back to the original question after giving evidence.

closingMoves.example: So yes, this is something I would love to be part of.
closingMoves.whenToUse: Conversational, confident close to a 'would you...?' answer.

closingMoves.example: Ultimately, learning to cook is not only useful but also helps children grow.
closingMoves.whenToUse: Summarises the long-term benefit or final takeaway.

closingMoves.example: Therefore, the hawker centre is an attractive place for people.
closingMoves.whenToUse: A formal, logical conclusion to wrap up an explanation.

upgradedExamples.weak: I think this is good because it helps the environment.
upgradedExamples.strong: Personally, I see this as a step in the right direction — recycling our worn-out items keeps them out of landfills and gives them a second life.
upgradedExamples.technique: Replace 'I think' with 'Personally,' + add a vivid 'second life' image
upgradedExamples.highlight: Personally, I see this as a step in the right direction

upgradedExamples.weak: I like making friends because it is fun.
upgradedExamples.strong: I enjoy making new friends as it allows me to widen my social network and interact with people who share similar interests.
upgradedExamples.technique: Upgrade basic vocabulary ('fun') to mature phrasing ('widen my social network')
upgradedExamples.highlight: allows me to widen my social network

upgradedExamples.weak: I don't like public speaking because I am shy.
upgradedExamples.strong: Being an introverted person, I tend to shy away from public speaking as I often get nervous and stumble over my words.
upgradedExamples.technique: Use character traits ('introverted person') and specific physical reactions ('stumble over my words')
upgradedExamples.highlight: Being an introverted person, I tend to shy away from

upgradedExamples.weak: I will tell my forgetful friend to write things down.
upgradedExamples.strong: If a friend tends to be forgetful, I would advise him or her to jot down their tasks in a notebook to serve as a handy checklist.
upgradedExamples.technique: Replace 'tell' with 'advise', and add a clear purpose ('serve as a handy checklist')
upgradedExamples.highlight: advise him or her to jot down their tasks

upgradedExamples.weak: I want school to start late so I can sleep more.
upgradedExamples.strong: If I had a choice, I would prefer school to start slightly later, as it would give heavy sleepers like me ample time to rest and recharge.
upgradedExamples.technique: Use a conditional opener ('If I had a choice') and idiomatic expressions ('heavy sleepers', 'rest and recharge')
upgradedExamples.highlight: give heavy sleepers like me ample time to rest and recharge

upgradedExamples.weak: I like reading books because they are interesting.
upgradedExamples.strong: I am an avid reader because books have the power to transport me to different worlds and broaden my horizons.
upgradedExamples.technique: Replace 'I like reading' with 'I am an avid reader' and use metaphorical language ('transport me to different worlds')
upgradedExamples.highlight: transport me to different worlds and broaden my horizons

upgradedExamples.weak: I think the poster is good to remind students.
upgradedExamples.strong: Yes, I believe the poster serves as an effective visual reminder, acting as a checklist for students before they leave the room.
upgradedExamples.technique: Upgrade 'is good to remind' to 'serves as an effective visual reminder'
upgradedExamples.highlight: serves as an effective visual reminder

upgradedExamples.weak: I like to go outdoors because the classroom is boring.
upgradedExamples.strong: I love spending my free time outdoors because being cooped up in a classroom all day can feel monotonous and suffocating.
upgradedExamples.technique: Replace 'boring' with stronger descriptive adjectives ('monotonous and suffocating') and idioms ('cooped up')
upgradedExamples.highlight: being cooped up in a classroom all day can feel monotonous
